LAW AND MOTION 301, SPECIALLY-APPEARING DEFENDANT WIRELESS MATRIX CORPORATION'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THE REQUIRED MINIMUM CONTACTS FROM WHICH HIS CLAIM ARISES. THE REQUEST FOR FURTHER CONTINUANCE IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF WAS GIVEN ADEQUATE TIME TO OBTAIN THE APPROPRIATE DISCOVERY. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING HELD ON DEFENDANT WIRELESS MATRIX USA, INC.'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO TAKE SUBSEQUENT DEPOSITION OF STEVEN BLAIR. RULING: THE COURT FULLY ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION AND REASONING OF P RO TEM JUDGE ARLENE BARTON AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. MR. BAIR SHALL BE PRODUCED FOR DEPOSITION FOR THE LIMITED PURPOSE OF QUESTIONS RELATED TO MR. BAIR'S CONVERSATIONS RELATING TO THE DECLARATIONS FILED BY JEFF DAO AND BARB LYON IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO WIRELESS MATRIX USA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE DEPOSITION WILL BE LIMITED TO 2 HOURS. NEITHER QUESTIONING REGARDING THE TWO DIFFERENT VERSIONS OF THE RESUME NOR PRODUCTION OF THE RESUME IS ALLOWED AS IT WAS NOT PART OF THE INITIAL MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

Notice Of Motion For Order To Take Subsequent Deposition Of Steven Blair; Memo P/A; Declaration
Off Calendar Mar-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Karen J. Kubin, Esq., from Morrison & Foerster LLP, 415-268-6168, for Defendant and moving party
Cari A. Cohorn, Esq., from Phillips, Erlewine & Given LLP, 415-398-0900, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Hearing held on DEFENDANT WIRELESS MATRIX USA, INC.'S Motion For Order To Take Subsequent Deposition Of Steven Blair.
Ruling: The court fully adopts the recommendation and reasoning of Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton as follows: The motion to compel is granted. Mr. Bair shall be produced for deposition for the limited purpose of questions related to Mr. Bair's conversations relating to the Declarations filed by Jeff Dao and Barb Lyon in support of plaintiff's opposition to Wireless Matrix USA, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. The deposition will be limited to 2 hours. Neither questioning regarding the two different versions of the resume nor production of the resume is allowed as it was not part of the initial motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Karen J. Kubin, Esq., from Morrison & Foerster LLP, 415-268-6168, for defendant and moving party
Cari A. Cohorn, Esq., and R. Scottt Erlewine, Esq., from Phillips, Erlewine & Given LLP, 415-398-0900, for plaintiff and opposing party

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WIRELESS MATRIX USA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied.
Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied.
The declarations of Mr. Dao and Ms. Lyon create triable issues regarding plaintiff's job duties and whether he was an exempt employee.
As to the Federal Computer Professional Exemption, matter is taken under submission. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
